text.title: lang-php-phpstan9-psalm-taint Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for lang-php-phpstan9-psalm-taint. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: CI MUST run vendor/bin/phpstan analyse --level=9 --memory-limit=1G on every PR; failures block merge. Pre-existing errors live in phpstan-baseline.neon (committed); new errors NEVER go into the baseline. Agents may not introduce mixed parameters, properties, or return types — annotate the actual type or return never.
rule.rationale: Level 9 turns LLM-friendly mixed returns (the lowest-effort plausible annotation) into compile-time failures, forcing agent output to converge on the actual type the code uses.

rule.statement: CI MUST run vendor/bin/psalm --taint-analysis --threads=4 on every PR; any taint issue blocks merge. Suppressing a taint (@psalm-taint-escape) is allowed ONLY when the call site applies a cited sanitizer, and the suppression line MUST include a comment naming the sanitizer (e.g. // taint-escape: htmlspecialchars).
rule.rationale: Type checkers cannot detect SQLi or XSS because the dangerous string is well-typed string; only data-flow analysis catches the pattern. Forcing a named-sanitizer comment on every escape makes the audit trail self-explanatory.
